title: translate.py: Position.altitude decoded as unsigned - negative altitudes incorrect
description:
File: python/src/lichen/interface/meshtastic/translate.py
Line: 139 (Position.from_bytes)

Bug: The _decode_varint() function returns unsigned values, but protobuf int32 field altitude can be negative (locations below sea level, e.g., Dead Sea at -430m). When decoded, negative altitudes become huge positive numbers.

Root cause: Line 258-269 _decode_varint() builds an unsigned value. Same issue as proto.py but in the separate translate.py varint decoder.

Impact: Positions below sea level show incorrect altitude in Meshtastic app.

Fix: Add signed int32 conversion in Position.from_bytes for altitude field:
```python
val, consumed = _decode_varint(data[i:])
if field_num == _POS_ALTITUDE:
    # int32 can be negative - reinterpret as signed
    if val > 0x7FFFFFFF:
        val -= 0x100000000
    pos.altitude = val
```
close_reason: Fixed: Truncate varint to 32 bits before signed conversion (protobuf encodes negative int32 as 10-byte sign-extended 64-bit). Added test for Dead Sea altitude -430m. All 18 translate tests pass.
